name: Ginger Honey Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fine graham cracker crumbs (about 8 full crackers)
1/4 cup Unsalted butter, cold (cut into 1/4 inch pieces)
1 lb Cream cheese, at room temperature
1 tbsp Candied ginger, or more (to taste)
recipeInstructions:
Butter the sides only of an 8-inch springform pan. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Make the crust by mixing the cracker crumbs and 1 tablespoon sugar in a bowl. Add the cold butter and rub it in until all of the crumbs are dampened with the fat. Distribute the crumbs loosely but evenly in the pan and press them in, pushing slightly up the sides. Cover and chill while you make the filling.
Using an electric mixer, cream the cream cheese, honey, and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add the remaining ingredients and continue to beat until evenly blended. Use more ginger for a pronounced flavor or less for a subtle effect.
Scrape the filling into the pan and bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes. Cool thoroughly on a rack (several hours), then cover, still in the pan, and refrigerate for 12 hours before unmolding and slicing.
Note: Ken Haedrich writes that baking cheesecakes in a water bath improves texture and may prevent top splitting.
